It feels inspiring, right? You feel if your solution gets implemented, it reflects positively on you. Calibrated how questions work off of this principle. They have the power to make your counterpart articulate how implementation will occur, in their own words, which in turn makes them believe their final solution was their idea. Human nature tells us people put more effort into something they feel personally invested in or responsible for. They're more likely to work really hard at seeing it through. There are two great questions you can ask the other party that will cause them to think they're defining success their way. The first is, how will we know we're on track? And the second is, how will we address things if we find we're off track? The key here is that after they respond, mirror them. Then summarize their answers until you get to that's right. Once you hear those two magic words, they're in. Hook, line, and sinker. It's critical you're on the lookout for the two telling signs of fake agreement. If you recall, we said earlier that when you hear you're right, it should raise a huge red flag. That response indicates that they're tired of what you have to say and they just want you to stop talking. Second, when you're pushing for implementation and they say, I'll try, this really means they plan to fail because they don't feel invested in the agreement or term at hand. If you hear either of these two phrases, the only way to counter is to dive right back in with a label like, it still seems like there's something here I'm not getting. Follow with an effective pause and then more calibrated how questions until they define what successful implementation will look like in their own words. Once you get them back to this point, summarize what they said and get to those two magic words. That's right. Remember, yes is nothing without how. Get the other side to feel like they are the problem solvers. It will feel like they are the masters of their own fate without realizing you're guiding them the whole way. Let them come up with a solution to your problems. When they feel like it was their idea, they will be more invested in the execution of a deal that has your desired result. Keep asking how and you will succeed.
